title: eriogonum annuum
intro: Eriogonum annuum, also known as Indian rice, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum annuum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum annuum can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
